name: OpenMM (GPU-Accelerated Molecular Dynamics)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT + LGPL for wrappers); Vijay Pande / Stanford

performance.notes: CUDA/OpenCL GPU backend; production MD requires GPU; comparable to GROMACS speed

agent_readiness.notes: GPU-accelerated molecular dynamics engine with Python API. from openmm import app. pdb = app.PDBFile('input.pdb'). forcefield = app.ForceField('amber14-all.xml', 'amber14/tip3pfb.xml'). system = forcefield.createSystem(pdb.topology). integrator = openmm.LangevinMiddleIntegrator(300*unit.kelvin, 1/unit.picosecond, 0.004*unit.picoseconds). simulation = app.Simulation(pdb.topology, system, integrator). simulation.context.setPositions(pdb.positions). simulation.minimizeEnergy(). simulation.step(10000). Reporters: DCDReporter, StateDataReporter. AMBER, CHARMM, OPLS force fields.

gotchas: Requires CUDA GPU for production speed; CPU is too slow for large proteins
Force field parameters must match topology exactly — setup errors are common
No built-in analysis — combine with MDAnalysis/MDTraj for trajectory analysis
No type annotations — poor IDE support
best_when: GPU-accelerated protein MD simulation with AMBER/CHARMM force fields
Custom force field development and testing
When Python-native MD engine is preferred over GROMACS/AMBER
avoid_when: Without GPU (use GROMACS CPU version which is more optimized)
Standard biomolecular simulations where GROMACS is faster
Large systems (>1M atoms) where GROMACS scales better
summary: OpenMM provides GPU-accelerated molecular dynamics with Python API. app.ForceField('amber14-all.xml'). createSystem(topology). LangevinMiddleIntegrator. simulation.minimizeEnergy().step(n). DCDReporter. MIT + LGPL by Vijay Pande/Stanford. Python-native MD with AMBER/CHARMM/OPLS force fields. GPU required for production speed. No built-in analysis — pair with MDAnalysis. Used as engine for HTMD, OpenPathSampling, etc.
